Records.description: Equal-mass two-loop sunrise (sunset/London-transport) integral with q1=k1, q2=k2-k1, q3=-k2-p; three master integrals {I_{110}, I_{111}, I_{211}}. Pre-canonical DE has singular points at x in {0,-1,-9}; in D=2-2eps the family is solved in eps-form via the elliptic curve E^cut, modular parameter tau, and iterated integrals of Eisenstein-type modular forms for Gamma_1(6). Closed-form qbar-expansion in eq. sunrise_final_qbar_expansion. Canonical example of an elliptic Feynman integral.
Records.familyName: equal-mass two-loop sunrise I_{nu1 nu2 nu3}
Records.location: Chapter 'Iterated integrals', Sec. 'Differential equations', Example 4 (line ~1386, eq. dgl_equal_mass_sunrise); Chapter 'Elliptics', Sec. 'Feynman integrals depending on one kinematic variable', eq. def_equal_mass_sunrise (line ~2891) and full solution lines ~2880-3700

Records.authors: Adams, Luise, Bogner, Christian, Weinzierl, Stefan
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise with equal masses in d=2-2*eps. All-order algorithm for eps expansion using elliptic ELi functions parametrized by the nome q.

Records.authors: Frellesvig, Hjalte, Gasparotto, Federico, Laporta, Stefano, Mandal, Manoj K., Mastrolia, Pierpaolo, Mattiazzi, Luca, Mizera, Sebastian
Records.description: Equal-mass massive two-loop sunrise (Broadhurst hep-ph/9304249, Laporta hep-ph/0410259) with three propagators of mass m=1 and one ISP z=k2^2 in the variable x=sqrt(s). Intersection-number reduction in the dlog basis yields 3 masters, reduced to 2 independent ones via a sunrise symmetry relation between dotted integrals. A second-order ODE is derived for the canonical-like form, and at d=2 the homogeneous solutions are explicit complete elliptic integrals K and E with modulus chi(x)=16 x/((x-1)^3(x+3)), exhibiting the elliptic structure of this graph.

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Dulat, Falko, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise integral with three equal massive propagators in d=2-2*eps dimensions. Master integrals S_{111} and S_{211} computed analytically in terms of elliptic multiple polylogarithms (eMPLs) by direct Feynman parameter integration. S_{110} is a product of one-loop integrals. Results given at O(eps^0) and O(eps^1) for S_{111}, O(eps^0) for S_{211}; algorithm extends to all orders in eps.

Two-loop equal-mass sunrise self-energy integral with three massive propagators in d=2-2*eps. Expressed to all orders in eps as iterated integrals of modular forms of Gamma_1(6). Alphabet: {1, f_2, f_3, f_4}. Three master integrals: S_110, S_111, S_211.

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Dulat, Falko, Penante, Brenda, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Two-loop equal-mass sunrise integral family in D=2-2eps with three master integrals computed in terms of elliptic multiple polylogarithms (pure eMPLs) of uniform transcendental weight two.

Records.authors: Bönisch, Kilian, Duhr, Claude, Fischbach, Fabian, Klemm, Albrecht, Nega, Christoph
Records.description: Two-loop equal-mass sunrise in d=2-2eps. Picard-Fuchs operator is 2nd order, singular at z=0,1/9,1,inf. D=2 result as iterated Eisenstein series for Gamma_1(6). Numerical to O(eps^2).

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Dulat, Falko, Marzucca, Robin, Penante, Brenda, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise graph with three equal non-zero internal masses, reviewed to establish connection with the banana family. 3 master integrals (2 non-trivial + 1 tadpole). Results in d=2 as iterated integrals of modular forms for Gamma_1(6).

Two-loop sunrise graph with three equal internal masses in d=2-2eps. The geometry is a single elliptic curve. Epsilon-factorised DE obtained via semi-simple part of period matrix; no extra functions G_i needed. The GM connection involves the holomorphic period varpi_0 but not its derivative.

Records.location: Eq. in Section 5.3 (line after 'Let us consider the equal-mass sunrise integral'), Section 5.3

Records.description: Two-loop equal-mass banana (sunrise) integral in D=2-2eps dimensions. Related to an elliptic curve (Calabi-Yau 1-fold). Picard-Fuchs operator is second order with singular points at x in {0,1,9,infinity}. Three master integrals. The eps-factorised differential equation was previously given in arXiv:1802.05020. The differential one-forms are modular forms.
